useCases.title: Social Media Profile Verification using Digital Identity Wallets
useCases.summary:
Social media and messaging platforms allow users to create profiles and interact with others without requiring formal identity verification. While this enables easy access to digital services, platforms and users may have limited assurance that a profile is associated with a verified individual.

RTI enables users to optionally verify their profile using the Aadhaar App or another compatible digital identity wallet. Users can continue to access the platform and use messaging services without verification. However, users who choose to verify their identity can securely present a verifiable identity credential from their wallet.

Once the credential is successfully verified, the user's profile displays a verified status indicating that the profile has been verified using a trusted digital identity credential. This provides an additional trust signal to other users without requiring the platform to collect or store copies of identity documents.

The use case demonstrates how digital identity wallets and verifiable credentials can introduce optional, privacy-preserving identity assurance into social platforms while preserving user choice and open access.
useCases.userJourney:
A user downloads Arattai, creates a profile, and can immediately access the platform's messaging and social features without completing identity verification.

If the user chooses to verify their profile, Arattai initiates an app-to-app verification request to the Aadhaar App or another compatible digital identity wallet, such as Google Wallet. The user reviews the identity request and provides consent to share the required credential.

The wallet presents a verifiable identity credential to Arattai. Arattai cryptographically verifies the credential and, once verification is successful, updates the user's profile to display a verified status.

Other users can then see that the profile has been verified using a trusted digital identity credential. The verification provides an additional layer of assurance without requiring Arattai to collect or store copies of the user's identity document.

useCases.title: Doctor Identity Verification for Medical Practitioner Registration
useCases.summary:
Medical councils verify doctors before granting registration to practise medicine. As part of the registration process, the Tamil Nadu Medical Council uses the Aadhaar App to verify the identity of MBBS graduates applying for a practitioner registration.

Applicants present their Aadhaar identity information through the Aadhaar App, allowing the Council to verify that the person applying is the legitimate holder of the identity and credentials submitted. This helps prevent impersonation or the misuse of another person's academic certificates to obtain a medical practitioner registration. Aadhaar verification forms one part of the Council's broader registration and credential verification process.
useCases.userJourney: An MBBS graduate applies for practitioner registration with the Tamil Nadu Medical Council. As part of the registration process, the applicant is asked to verify their identity using the Aadhaar App. The applicant presents their Aadhaar identity information and completes proof of presence through face verification. The Council verifies the presented identity and uses the verified information as part of its broader checks on the applicant's academic and professional credentials. Once the required checks are completed, the Council proceeds with the practitioner registration process.
